S Skillmaker Studio
Docs GitHub

the pull

Your agent did something weird again.

No crash. No error. Nothing in the logs — just off, in front of someone who matters. And you can't say why, which version did it, or what changed.

the enemy

It's not you. It's entropy.

Every skill you ship goes out into a world that never stops changing it: new models on someone else's schedule, teammates with edit access, runtimes it never met. Skills don't fail like servers. They rot quietly — and the weirdness lands on you.

the machine

Skillmaker Studio is the machine that crushes entropy.

It turns drift into a number, blame into a lookup, and breakage into a work order. Receipts on every skill — what it passed, on which version, on which model. When the world moves, you know first.

watch it happen

Bring the problem child in first.

You have one — the skill everyone works around. Here's its week in the studio, and what happens after.

  1. 01

    $ skillmaker adopt

    That skill everyone quietly distrusts? Bring it into the studio first. Nothing moves — it becomes measurable right where it lives.

  2. 02

    what does “good” mean?

    You and the agent write down what it's actually supposed to do — evals in plain English. For most skills, this is the first time it's ever been written down.

  3. 03

    $ skillmaker run

    The first honest number. Lower than you hoped — and the first time you can see the flake instead of just feeling it.

  4. 04

    the operating table

    Trail each failure back to the choice that caused it. Fix the process, not just the prompt.

  5. 05

    green, with a receipt

    Re-measure. The number climbs, pinned to the exact version and model that earned it. The problem child has a report card.

  6. 06

    now the rest of them

    Adopt the whole repo. Every skill gets the same treatment; every new skill is born this way. Next time entropy swings, the board tells you — not a customer.

the secret

The secret is the trail.

Every number is pinned to the exact skill version and model that earned it. Every design choice is linked to the evidence behind it. So when a skill is stable but wrong — running clean, doing the job badly — you can see it, trace it, and fix the process, not just the prompt.

Entropy doesn't stop. With the trail, it stops mattering.

the pain board

You know these. Or you're waiting on them.

Model just got updated.

Re-run the receipts. Know what survived by lunch.

Skill drifted.

The number moved and you found out first. The trail shows what changed.

Someone else made it. Is it any good?

Adopt it, measure it, get an answer with a confidence interval.

Was working great. Just did something crazy.

Version, model, trail — reconstruct why in minutes, not a postmortem.

A teammate “improved” it. Same name.

Your version passed on the model it was rated for. The failure is theirs, provably.

It's running on models it was never tested on.

Receipts are per-model. Untested reads untested — loudly.

It's due Friday and it has to be good.

Design, measure, fix, re-measure — one surface, plain English.

Thirty skills. Which ones still work?

Adopt the repo. The board answers, skill by skill.

Same enemy every time. Same machine every time.

you're already objecting. good.

“Do we have to restructure our repo?”

No. adopt works in place — 59/60 and 39/39 skills adopted cleanly on real public repos. Zero files moved.

“I'm not an evals person.”

You don't need to be. Our agents co-design the evals with you, in plain English. You judge; they build.

“Won't the numbers flatter you?”

They refuse to. Three runs reads “below smoke” on our own case study — even when the percentage looks great.

“Where does my data go?”

Nowhere. Free, local, MIT-licensed, no account. It phones nothing home.

the next ninety seconds

Find out first.

ninety seconds 
$ curl -fsSL https://skillmaker.studio/install.sh | sh
$ skillmaker adopt

Free. Local. MIT. Phones nothing home. On the repo you already have.

Want hands-on help? We're working with a small first round of design partners — open an issue and tell us what broke. Running skills as a team? Talk to us — we're designing the multi-player studio with a handful of companies.

We can't make your agent predictable — nobody can; that's what you hired it for. We can make sure nothing drifts silently, nothing fails anonymously, and nothing stays wrong.

The only thing you risk is finding out.
